     The Numbers Business Report

The Way Back (2011)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $2,719,296Details
International Box Office $23,805,693
Worldwide Box Office $26,524,989
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Based on Slavomir Rawicz's memoir, The Way Back is a story of escape from a Siberian gulag and the subsequent 4000 mile walk to freedom in India.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$1,218,867 (44.8% of total gross)
Legs:2.23 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:10.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Theater counts:678 opening theaters/678 max. theaters, 2.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $4,101,228

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 29th, 2010 (Oscar Qualifying Run) by Newmarket Films
January 21st, 2011 (Wide) by Newmarket Films
International Releases: July 6th, 2012 (Wide) (Italy)
Video Release: April 19th, 2011 by Image Entertainment/ThinkFilm
MPA Rating: PG-13 for violent content, depiction of physical hardships, a nude image and brief strong language.
(Rating bulletin 2142, 10/13/2010)
Running Time: 133 minutes
Plot point: Escape, Road Trip
Social setting: World War II
Source material: Based on an UNTRUE Story
Subgenre: Survival Adventure
Time period setting: 1940s
Source:Based on Factual Book/Article
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Exclusive Media Group, National Geographic Entertainment, Image Nation Abu Dhabi
Production Countries: United Kingdom, United Arab Emirates
Languages: English, Russian, Polish
